sections.text: For years, Byju's was the household name in Indian edtech. Parents across the country trusted the platform to supplement their children's school learning with animated video lessons, practice tests, and a polished app experience. But 2025 and 2026 have been a turning point. Financial troubles, mass layoffs, acquisition controversies, and a growing chorus of unhappy parents have pushed thousands of families to search for the best Byju's alternative for their kids.

If you are one of those parents, you are not alone. Google searches for "Byju's alternative" have surged over 300% in the last twelve months. And the good news is that the Indian edtech space has matured. There are excellent platforms that offer live classes, real mentors, hands-on coding, and integrated maths at a fraction of what Byju's used to charge.

In this guide, we will cover everything: why parents are leaving, what to look for in a replacement, a head-to-head comparison of six major platforms, and why we believe Modern Age Coders is the strongest Byju's alternative available today. No marketing spin, just an honest, data-backed comparison so you can make the right choice for your child.

sections.alt: Parent comparing online education platforms on a laptop while a child learns coding on a tablet in the background
sections.caption: More parents are actively comparing platforms before committing to long-term plans

sections.text: Why Parents Are Looking for Byju's Alternatives in 2026

Before we get into comparisons, it is worth understanding what is driving the exodus. These are not minor complaints. They are structural issues that affect the learning experience of millions of children.
1. The Financial Chaos and Trust Deficit

Byju's has faced severe financial scrutiny since late 2023. Delayed financial filings, failed acquisitions of companies like Aakash and Great Learning, mass layoffs affecting thousands of employees, and legal battles with creditors have shaken parent confidence. When a company cannot pay its own teachers on time, parents rightly wonder whether their child's learning experience is a priority. Trust, once broken, is hard to rebuild.
2. Recorded Videos Are Not Enough

Byju's core product is pre-recorded video lessons. These are well-produced, no doubt. But here is the problem: a seven-year-old watching a video is not the same as a seven-year-old working through a problem with a live mentor who can answer questions in real time. Research consistently shows that active learning, where students do, build, and interact, produces far better outcomes than passive watching. Children need someone who notices when they are confused, adjusts the pace, and celebrates their breakthroughs. A video cannot do that.

sections.alt: Comparison showing a child passively watching a recorded video versus a child actively participating in a live coding class with a mentor
sections.caption: Live 1:1 classes lead to 3x better retention compared to recorded video content

sections.text: 3. Aggressive Sales Tactics and Expensive Packages

This is perhaps the most common complaint in Byju's reviews across the internet. Parents report being pressured into buying multi-year packages costing Rs 50,000 to Rs 1,50,000 or more. Many were offered tablet bundles with EMI loans that were difficult to cancel. Sales representatives would call repeatedly, sometimes pushing parents who clearly could not afford the packages. The NCPCR (National Commission for Protection of Child Rights) itself flagged some of these practices. Education should be about the child, not about locking parents into expensive contracts.
4. No Coding Curriculum Worth Mentioning

In 2026, coding is no longer optional. The NEP 2020 has made computational thinking part of the school curriculum from Class 6. Companies are hiring coders at record rates. Yet Byju's has never offered a serious, structured coding program. Their focus has always been exam preparation: CBSE, ICSE, JEE, NEET. While exam prep has its place, it does not prepare children for the future the way learning to code, building projects, and developing computational thinking does. Parents who want both academic support and future-ready skills need a platform that offers both.
5. One-Size-Fits-All Approach

Every child learns differently. Some grasp concepts quickly and get bored with repetition. Others need more time and hands-on practice. Byju's video-based model delivers the exact same content to every student at the same pace. There is no personalization of the kind you get when a live teacher adapts in real time to how your child is responding. The best learning happens when instruction meets the child where they are, not where a pre-recorded script assumes they are.
What to Look for in a Byju's Alternative
Before you jump to another platform, here is a checklist of what actually matters. Use this to evaluate any option, including the ones we discuss below.

items: Live classes with real teachers — Not just recorded videos. Your child should be able to ask questions and get answers in real time.
1-on-1 or small group format — Batch sizes of 20-50 are just school classrooms on a screen. Look for 1-on-1 or groups of 3-5 students maximum.
Coding + Maths together — These subjects reinforce each other. A platform that offers both saves money and produces deeper learning.
Age-appropriate curriculum — A 6-year-old and a 16-year-old need completely different approaches. The platform should have separate tracks.
Transparent pricing — No hidden fees, no multi-year lock-ins, no EMI pressure. Monthly plans with the freedom to cancel anytime.
Real projects and portfolios — The child should be building things, not just answering MCQs. Look for project-based learning with shareable outcomes.
Qualified instructors — Professional teachers with real subject expertise, not college students reading from scripts.
Global curriculum — Especially important if you want education that is relevant beyond Indian board exams.
Free trial or demo — Any confident platform will let your child try a real class before you pay anything.

sections.alt: Checklist infographic showing key features to evaluate when choosing an online learning platform for kids including live classes, coding, maths, and transparent pricing
sections.caption: Use this checklist when comparing any online learning platform

sections.text: Top 6 Byju's Alternatives Compared

We have evaluated six platforms that parents commonly consider as Byju's alternatives. Here is an honest, feature-by-feature comparison covering what matters most: class format, subjects, pricing, and overall value.
1. Modern Age Coders — Best Overall Alternative

Modern Age Coders is the world's first platform that integrates coding and mathematics into a single, live-taught curriculum. Founded by educators who saw the gap between exam-focused learning and real-world skill building, the platform serves students aged 6 to 65 across 15+ countries. Every class is live, interactive, and taught by professional instructors with industry experience.

items: Class format: Live 1-on-1 and small group classes (3-5 students)
Subjects: Coding (Scratch, Python, JavaScript, Java, HTML/CSS, C++) + Mathematics (Class 1-12, college, advanced)
Special programs: Code Queens (girls-only batches with female mentors), AI/ML course, game development, app development
Tools taught: Pygame, Roblox Studio, MIT App Inventor, ChatGPT, Teachable Machine, Canva, Figma, p5.js
Pricing: Rs 1,499/month (group) | Rs 2,499/month (1-on-1) — no contracts, cancel anytime
Free demo: Yes, full class with a real instructor — book here
Best for: Parents who want coding + maths in one place, with live expert instruction at an affordable price

sections.text: Modern Age Coders is the only platform on this list that offers both coding and maths in an integrated curriculum with live 1-on-1 classes. If your child needs support in both subjects, this is the most cost-effective and educationally sound choice.
2. WhiteHat Jr

Acquired by Byju's in 2020, WhiteHat Jr was one of the first platforms to popularize coding education for kids in India. It offers Scratch and Python courses through live classes. However, the platform has faced its own share of controversies including inflated marketing claims and high pricing.

items: Class format: Group batches (4-10 students), 1-on-1 available at premium
Subjects: Coding only (Scratch, Python). No maths.
Pricing: Rs 5,000 - Rs 10,000+ per month depending on plan
Contracts: 6-12 month packages typical, difficult to cancel
Best for: Families who only want basic Scratch/Python for younger kids and do not mind paying a premium

sections.text: 3. Vedantu

Vedantu is a strong contender for academic tuition, particularly for board exam preparation (CBSE, ICSE) and competitive exams (JEE, NEET). Their WAVE platform provides a live, interactive classroom experience that is better than pure recorded content. However, Vedantu is primarily a school syllabus and exam prep platform. It does not offer structured coding courses.

items: Class format: Live group classes (can be large batches), some 1-on-1 options
Subjects: School subjects (Maths, Science, English), JEE/NEET prep. No coding.
Pricing: Rs 2,000 - Rs 8,000+ per month depending on course and format
Best for: Students who need focused exam preparation and school subject tuition

sections.text: 4. Toppr (now part of Byju's ecosystem)

Toppr was an adaptive learning platform known for its practice question bank and doubt-solving feature. After being acquired by Byju's, much of its original identity has merged into the larger Byju's ecosystem. The platform uses AI-driven personalized learning paths but relies heavily on self-study with recorded content rather than live instruction.

items: Class format: Primarily self-paced with doubt-solving support, some live sessions
Subjects: School subjects, competitive exam prep. No coding.
Pricing: Varies, often bundled with Byju's packages
Best for: Self-motivated students who learn well independently and need an extensive question bank

sections.text: 5. Unacademy

Unacademy has carved out a strong position in competitive exam preparation, especially for UPSC, JEE, NEET, and state-level exams. Their subscription model provides access to thousands of live and recorded sessions from popular educators. For K-12 school subjects, they have limited but growing coverage. Like Vedantu and Toppr, Unacademy does not offer coding courses for kids.

items: Class format: Live and recorded large-group sessions, some 1-on-1 mentoring for premium subscribers
Subjects: Competitive exams (JEE, NEET, UPSC), some K-12. No coding for kids.
Pricing: Rs 3,000 - Rs 7,000+ per month for subscription plans
Best for: Older students preparing for competitive exams like JEE, NEET, or UPSC

sections.text: 6. Code.org

Code.org is a fantastic free resource for introducing children to basic coding concepts. Their Hour of Code activities and structured courses are used in schools worldwide. However, Code.org is entirely self-paced with no live instruction, no personal mentorship, and no maths integration. It works well as a supplement but is not a replacement for structured, mentor-led education.

items: Class format: Self-paced only. No live classes, no instructors.
Subjects: Basic coding (block-based and some JavaScript). No maths.
Pricing: Completely free
Best for: Absolute beginners who want to explore coding casually before committing to a structured program

Here is a detailed feature comparison of all six platforms against Byju's. This table covers the criteria that matter most to parents making this decision.

Notice that Modern Age Coders is the only platform in this comparison that offers coding and maths together, live 1-on-1 classes, no long-term contracts, and a dedicated girls program. At Rs 1,499-2,499 per month, it is also the most affordable paid option.
Live Classes vs Recorded Videos — Why It Matters
This is the single biggest difference between Byju's and platforms like Modern Age Coders, and it deserves its own section because it fundamentally changes learning outcomes.

Byju's built its entire model around high-production-value recorded videos. These videos are engaging to watch, but watching is not learning. The educational research is clear: students retain approximately 5% of what they hear in a lecture, 10% of what they read, but 75% of what they practice and 90% of what they teach others (National Training Laboratories, Learning Pyramid). A live coding class where a child writes code, debugs errors, and builds projects falls squarely in the high-retention zone.

sections.alt: Learning retention pyramid showing 5% retention from lectures, 10% from reading, 75% from practice, and 90% from teaching others
sections.caption: The Learning Pyramid shows why hands-on coding classes produce better outcomes than recorded videos

sections.text: Here is what live classes give your child that recorded videos cannot:

items: Instant doubt resolution — When a child is stuck on a concept, they can ask immediately instead of rewinding a video and hoping to figure it out alone.
Adaptive pacing — A live teacher notices when a child is bored (moving too slow) or confused (moving too fast) and adjusts in real time.
Accountability — Having a scheduled class with a teacher who knows your child's name creates structure and motivation that self-paced content cannot match.
Social-emotional learning — Children learn confidence, communication, and persistence through interaction with a mentor, not by watching a screen.
Real debugging skills — In coding, half the learning happens when something breaks. A live teacher guides the child through finding and fixing errors, which is how real developers work.

sections.text: At Modern Age Coders, every single class is live. There are no pre-recorded lessons masquerading as coursework. Your child works with the same instructor over time, building a relationship that makes learning feel personal and safe. This consistency is something parents regularly cite as the reason they stay.
The Coding + Maths Advantage — Why This Combo Matters

Most parents think of coding and maths as separate subjects. One is about computers, the other is about numbers. But at a fundamental level, coding is applied mathematics. And when children learn them together, both subjects click faster and stick longer.
Here is how they reinforce each other in a typical Modern Age Coders class:

items: Loops and sequences — A Python for loop is a mathematical sequence. When a child writes for i in range(1, 11), they are exploring number patterns.
Game coordinates — Building a game in Scratch or Pygame requires understanding the X-Y coordinate system, which is coordinate geometry from Class 8-9.
Variables and algebra — Every variable in code is an algebraic concept. score = score + 10 is literally an algebraic expression.
Angles and shapes — Drawing with Turtle graphics teaches angles, degrees, and geometric properties hands-on.
Logic and proofs — If-else conditions in code are logical statements. Debugging a program is essentially constructing a logical proof of correctness.
Data and statistics — Working with lists, averages, and data visualization in Python maps directly to statistics curriculum.
Probability — Building dice games or random event generators teaches probability through code.

sections.alt: Infographic showing how coding concepts like loops, variables, and coordinates map directly to mathematical concepts like sequences, algebra, and geometry
sections.caption: Coding and maths are two sides of the same coin — learning them together multiplies the benefit

sections.text: Byju's teaches maths through videos and MCQs. Vedantu and Unacademy teach it through exam-focused drills. WhiteHat Jr and Code.org do not teach it at all. Modern Age Coders is the only platform that weaves maths and coding together so that your child sees how abstract mathematical concepts come alive in real projects. Check our courses to see the full integrated curriculum.

sections.text: What Makes Modern Age Coders the Best Byju's Alternative
We have covered the comparison. Now let us go deeper into what specifically makes Modern Age Coders stand out as the best alternative for parents leaving Byju's.
Every Class Is Live and Interactive

No recorded videos. No self-paced modules with a chatbot for doubts. Every single session at Modern Age Coders is a live class with a real instructor who teaches, listens, and adapts. Whether your child is in a 1-on-1 session or a small group of 3-5 students, the teacher knows their name, their strengths, and their areas for improvement. This is the kind of personal attention that Byju's model simply cannot provide.
Integrated Coding + Maths Curriculum (No One Else Offers This)

This is our core differentiator. We are not a coding platform that bolted on some maths worksheets. Our curriculum was designed from the ground up to show students how coding and mathematics are deeply connected. When a student learns about angles in maths, they also draw shapes using Python Turtle. When they learn about variables, they use them in a real game. This dual-reinforcement approach produces students who understand concepts at a much deeper level than either subject taught in isolation. Explore the full curriculum on our courses page.
Six Programming Languages and Real Tools

Byju's teaches zero programming languages. WhiteHat Jr stops at Scratch and Python. At Modern Age Coders, students progress through Scratch, Python, JavaScript, HTML/CSS, Java, and C++ based on their age and level. They also work with industry tools including Pygame, Roblox Studio, MIT App Inventor, Thunkable, ChatGPT, Teachable Machine, Canva, Figma, and p5.js. When your child finishes one level, there is always a next step. They never outgrow the platform.
Ages 6 to 65 — A Platform for the Whole Family

Byju's focuses on K-12 students. Modern Age Coders serves kids (6-12), teens (13-18), college students, and working professionals. We have parents who signed up their children and then enrolled themselves. Our corporate training programs help professionals upskill in Python, data science, and AI. This range means that as your child grows, the platform grows with them. And if you have always wanted to learn coding yourself, you can do it on the same platform.
Code Queens — Girls-Only Program

Globally, women make up only 28% of the tech workforce. That number is even lower in India. Our Code Queens program offers all-girls batches with female mentors, creating a supportive environment where young women build confidence in technology without the social pressures of mixed classrooms. No other platform on this list, including Byju's, offers anything like this.
Project-Based Learning with Real Portfolios

Byju's measures progress through tests and scores. We measure it through what your child builds. Every student creates real projects: games, websites, apps, chatbots, and data visualizations. These projects go into a shareable portfolio that they can show to schools, colleges, and eventually employers. Learning to code by building real things is not just more effective, it is also more fun. And fun matters when you want a child to stick with learning for years.

sections.text: Global Reach, Flexible Scheduling

We have students in 15+ countries including India, the US, UK, UAE, Singapore, Australia, and Canada. Classes are scheduled flexibly across all time zones, and the curriculum is globally relevant, not tied to any single country's exam board. Whether you are in Delhi or Dubai, your child gets the same world-class experience.
How to Choose the Right Platform for Your Child
Every child is different, and every family has different priorities. Here is a quick decision framework to help you choose.
Choose Vedantu or Unacademy if...

Your primary goal is board exam preparation or competitive exam coaching (JEE, NEET, UPSC). These platforms specialize in academic test prep and have strong content libraries for those use cases. However, they will not teach your child to code.
Choose Code.org if...

You want a free, casual introduction to coding for a young child who has never tried it before. It is an excellent starting point, but your child will outgrow it quickly and need structured live instruction.
Choose WhiteHat Jr if...

You specifically want a brand name you have heard of and are comfortable paying Rs 5,000-10,000+ per month for coding only. Be aware that the curriculum is limited to Scratch and Python, and there is no maths component.
Choose Modern Age Coders if...

You want the best value for money with live 1-on-1 instruction in both coding and maths, a wide range of programming languages and tools, no long-term contracts, and a platform your child will not outgrow. This is the clear choice for parents who want future-ready skills alongside strong academic foundations.

Not sure which platform is right? The simplest test is a free demo class. Let your child experience a real session with a real instructor and see how they respond. If they come out of it excited and asking when the next class is, you have your answer. Book a free demo with Modern Age Coders.

When we switched to Modern Age Coders, the change was immediate. His teacher connected maths concepts to coding projects, and for the first time, my son said 'I actually get why algebra matters.' He is now learning Python and his school maths grades have gone from B to A. Worth every rupee."

Meera R., Mother of 7-year-old, Hyderabad: "I was worried about screen time with another online platform. But the difference is that on Byju's my daughter was passively watching, while at Modern Age Coders she is actively creating. She talks to her teacher, asks questions, and shows me what she built after every class. She actually gets excited about her coding class. That never happened with Byju's."
Frequently Asked Questions

items.title: Is Modern Age Coders better than Byju's for kids?
items.content: It depends on what you are looking for. If you want live 1-on-1 classes, coding education, integrated maths, project-based learning, and affordable pricing without long-term contracts, then yes, Modern Age Coders is a significantly better option. Byju's strength was in pre-recorded video content for exam preparation, but the platform's recent challenges and the limitations of recorded content have pushed many families to seek live, interactive alternatives.

items.title: How much can I save by switching from Byju's to Modern Age Coders?
items.content: Most families report saving Rs 5,000 to Rs 12,000+ per month. If you were also paying separately for coding classes (like WhiteHat Jr), the savings are even larger since Modern Age Coders includes both coding and maths in the same subscription. Our group classes are Rs 1,499/month, Mini Batch (3-4 students) is Rs 2,499/month, and 1-on-1 sessions are Rs 4,999/month with no contracts.

items.title: Does Modern Age Coders help with school maths and board exams?
items.content: Yes. Our maths curriculum covers Class 1 through Class 12 for both CBSE and ICSE boards. The difference is that we teach maths concepts through practical application and coding projects, not just rote memorization. Students develop a deeper understanding that helps with both board exams and real-world problem solving.

items.title: My child has never coded before. Can they still join?
items.content: Absolutely. We have beginner tracks designed for children as young as 6 who have never written a line of code. We start with visual, block-based coding (Scratch) and gradually move to text-based languages as the child builds confidence. Your child will write their first program in their very first class.

items.title: Are the classes really live or are they recorded sessions?
items.content: Every class at Modern Age Coders is 100% live. There are no pre-recorded videos, no auto-play lessons. Your child interacts with a real instructor in real time who adapts to their learning pace. We also maintain instructor consistency so your child builds a relationship with their teacher over time.

items.title: Can I cancel anytime? Are there any contracts?
items.content: There are zero contracts, zero lock-in periods. You pay month to month and can cancel, pause, or resume anytime with no questions asked and no penalties. We believe that if our classes are good, you will stay because you want to, not because a contract forces you to.

items.title: Do you offer classes for teens and adults too?
items.content: Yes! Our platform serves ages 6 to 65. We have separate curriculum tracks for kids (6-12), teens (13-18), college students, and working professionals. Parents often enroll themselves after seeing their children enjoy the classes. We teach Python, JavaScript, Java, data science, AI/ML, and web development for adult learners.

items.title: How do I switch from Byju's to Modern Age Coders?
items.content: Simply book a free demo class at learn.modernagecoders.com/book-demo. Your child will experience a real lesson with one of our instructors. If you are happy, you can start immediately with a monthly plan. We will assess your child's current level and place them at the right stage in our curriculum. There is no need to start from scratch if they already have some knowledge.
